description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figures of speech, how words are related, and the slight differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what unfamiliar or multi-meaning words mean by using clues from the sentence, breaking down word parts, or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use language differently depending on the situation, to make their meaning or style clear, and to better understand what they read or hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, both for longer projects and for shorter assignments, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw and cite evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence from stories or informational texts to support their ideas, reflections, or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources; assess the credibility of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and providing basic bibliographic information for sources.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from different sources, check if those sources are trustworthy, and use the information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources, and refocusing the inquiry when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer questions, using information from several sources and changing their focus if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to link to and cite sources, as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, publish, give credit to sources, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the assignment, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that makes sense for the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses specific words, details, and descriptions that help readers imagine the experiences or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child uses storytelling techniques like dialogue, pacing, and description to develop the story or characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene, introducing the main character or narrator, and organizing events in a way that makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events, using good writing techniques, clear details, and a logical order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a strong ending that fits with the information or explanation they have given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain an appropriate style for content, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child will use a writing style that matches the topic, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific words, including subject-related vocabulary, to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to connect ideas and show how they relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic by adding facts, definitions, examples, quotes, and other details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting, graphics, and multimedia when useful in aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic, organize their ideas clearly, and use things like headings, pictures, or videos to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative pieces that clearly explain a topic by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that matches and wraps up their argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain an appropriate style for content, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child will use a writing style that fits the topic, purpose, and audience when making an argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to clarify the relationships among claim(s) and reasons.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to clearly show how their reasons support their main point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with clear reasons and relevant evidence, using credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child can back up their opinions with clear reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) and organize the reasons and evidence clearly.
description_plain: Your child can state their main point and organize their reasons and evidence in a clear way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their opinions with clear reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will practice writing often, both for longer projects and quick assignments, for different purposes and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from books or articles to support their own ideas, research, or reflections.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find information from different sources, check if it is reliable and accurate, and use it in their own work without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do research projects, both short and long, by asking focused questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology, like computers and the Internet, to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the assignment and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events, using good writing techniques, clear details, and a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative pieces that clearly and accurately explain complex ideas by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their opinions with good reasons and enough evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6–8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand nonfiction books and articles written for grades 6–8, with help if needed for harder texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast one author's presentation of events with that of another.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two authors describe the same events in different ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can follow and judge the arguments in a text, telling which claims are supported by reasons and evidence and which are not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information presented in different media or formats as well as in words to develop a coherent understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from different sources, like videos, charts, and written texts, to better understand a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's perspective and/or purpose in a text and explain how it is conveyed in the text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why an author wrote something and explain how the author shows their point of view in the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter, or section fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different parts of a text fit together and help develop the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or descriptive meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail how a key individual, event, or idea is introduced, illustrated, and elaborated in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain in detail how a key person, event, or idea is introduced and explained in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how details support it, and summarize the text without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze what the text says explicitly as well as inferentially; cite textual evidence to support the analysis.
description_plain: Your child can explain what a text says directly and indirectly, and use evidence from the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 6–8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems written for grades 6–8, with help if needed for harder texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast how different types of texts handle similar themes and topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature.
description_plain: Not applicable to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the experience of reading a story, drama, or poem to listening to or viewing an audio, video, or live version of the text, including contrasting what they "see" and "hear" when reading the text to what they perceive when they listen or watch.
description_plain: Your child can compare reading a story, play, or poem to listening to or watching it, noticing the differences in what they see and hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author develops the point of view or perspective of the narrator or speaker in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows the narrator's or speaker's point of view in a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the theme, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a specific sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the whole story and helps build the main idea, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including those that are used in creative or technical ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a particular literary text's plot unfolds in a series of episodes as well as how the characters respond or change as the plot moves toward a resolution.
description_plain: Your child can describe how the story unfolds in different parts and how the characters react or change as the story moves toward the ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea or theme of a story and explain how it is shown through details, and can summarize the story without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze what the text says explicitly as well as inferentially; cite textual evidence to support the analysis.
description_plain: Your child can explain what the text says directly and what it hints at, using examples from the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about similar topics or themes and notice how the authors' approaches are alike or different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can identify and judge the arguments and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense and are strong enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information that is shown in different ways, such as through pictures, numbers, or words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is included and how the text is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the parts of a text, like sentences, paragraphs, or chapters, fit together and relate to the whole.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand what words and phrases mean in a text, including technical or creative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the meaning or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how and why people, events, or ideas change and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks demonstrating command of 7th grade level language conventions when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can adjust how they speak for different situations and use correct 7th grade language when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components and visual displays in presentations to clarify claims and findings and emphasize salient points.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, videos, or other visuals in presentations to help explain their ideas and highlight important points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and/or findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with pertinent descriptions, facts, details, and examples; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can give clear and focused presentations, using facts and examples, and speak with good eye contact, volume, and pronunciation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker's argument and claims, and judge if the reasons and evidence make sense and are strong.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the main ideas and supporting details presented in diverse media and formats, and explain how the ideas clarify a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can understand the main ideas and details from information shown in different ways, and explain how these ideas help explain a topic or issue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others and, when warranted, modify their own views.
description_plain: Your child can listen to new information from others and change their own opinions when it makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that elicit elaboration and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant observations and ideas that bring the discussion back on topic as needed.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions that encourage others to explain more, and respond to others with comments that keep the discussion on track.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow discussion rules, keep track of goals and deadlines, and help define roles in group work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes prepared for discussions by reading or researching, and uses evidence from their preparation to share and reflect on ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 7 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions on 7th grade topics, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak for different situations and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital media and visual aids to share information and make presentations clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information and evidence in a way that is organized and easy for listeners to follow, using the right style for the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can judge a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can understand and judge information presented in different ways, such as through pictures, numbers, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can get ready for and join in conversations with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly and convincingly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new grade-level words, including subject-specific terms, and look up meanings when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the feelings or ideas suggested by words that have similar basic meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use how words are related to each other to better understand what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech in context.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of figures of speech (like metaphors or similes) in context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can double-check to make sure they have found the correct meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses, as well as other general and specialized reference materials, both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, and thesauruses (in print or online) to find out how to say a word, what it means, or what part of speech it is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin word parts to help figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the surrounding text to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 7 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words and phrases in their reading by using different helpful strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose language that expresses ideas precisely and concisely, recognizing and eliminating wordiness and redundancy.
description_plain: Your child will choose words that clearly and simply express their ideas, avoiding extra or repeated words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use language rules correctly when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate coordinate adjectives.
description_plain: Your child will use a comma to separate two adjectives that both describe the same noun.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Place phrases and clauses within a sentence, recognizing and correcting misplaced and dangling modifiers.
description_plain: Your child can put phrases and clauses in the right place in a sentence and fix sentences where they are in the wrong spot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose among simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences to signal differing relationships among ideas.
description_plain: Your child will choose different types of sentences (simple, compound, complex) to show how ideas are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of phrases and clauses in general and their function in specific sentences.
description_plain: Your child can explain what phrases and clauses do in a sentence and how they work in specific examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a wide range of general and subject-specific words, and can figure out the meaning of new important words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and can explain figures of speech, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking words into parts, or looking them up in reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts,to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child knows how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles that fit what they are reading, writing, or listening to.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, both for longer projects that take time and for shorter assignments, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw and cite evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence from stories or informational texts to support their ideas, reflections, or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from books and online sources, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions for further research and investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer a question, using several sources and coming up with new, related questions to explore.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and link to and cite sources as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child uses technology, like the Internet, to write, publish, and share their work, and to work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to make sure it fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the assignment, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that makes sense and reflects on the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses specific words, details, and sensory language to help the reader picture what is happening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or setting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child uses storytelling techniques like dialogue, pacing, and description to develop the story and its characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view or perspective and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child starts their story by setting the scene, introducing the narrator or characters, and organizing events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, clear details, and a well-organized sequence of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes an ending that fits and supports the information or explanation they have given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain an appropriate style for content, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child keeps the writing style appropriate for the topic, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain the topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses transitions to connect ideas and make their writing flow smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child explains their topic using facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting, graphics, and multimedia when useful in aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child clearly introduces the topic, organizes information in a logical way, and uses things like headings, graphics, or multimedia to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative pieces that explain a topic clearly by choosing, organizing, and analyzing the right information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes an ending that fits and supports the argument they have made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain an appropriate style for content, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child keeps the writing style appropriate for the topic, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to connect their claim, reasons, and evidence so the writing is clear and organized.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child supports their argument with logical reasons and evidence from reliable sources, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their main argument, mention other viewpoints, and organize their reasons and evidence in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their ideas with clear reasons and evidence that makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly, sometimes working on longer projects and sometimes on shorter assignments, for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from books or articles to support their own ideas, reflections, or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from different sources, check if those sources are trustworthy, and use the information without copying it directly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do both short and longer research projects by asking focused questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new way to present their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the assignment, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events, using good details and organizing the events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain complex ideas or information clearly and accurately, using good organization and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about important topics or texts, using strong reasons and enough evidence to support their points.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6-8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand nonfiction books and articles written for grades 6-8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more authors writing about the same topic shape their presentations of key information by emphasizing different evidence or advancing different interpretations of facts.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different authors write about the same topic, noticing what evidence they use and how they interpret facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims.
description_plain: Your child can follow and judge the arguments in a text, deciding if the reasons and evidence really support the claims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a text to an audio, video, or multimedia version of the text, analyzing each medium's portrayal of the subject.
description_plain: Your child can compare a written text to its audio, video, or multimedia version, noticing how each one shows the subject differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's perspective, and/or purpose in a text and analyze how the author distinguishes his or her position from that of others.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's point of view or purpose and see how the author shows their position compared to others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can examine how an author organizes a text and how each section helps develop the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative uses, and see how word choices affect meaning and tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the interactions between individuals, events, and ideas in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas in a text affect each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more central ideas in a text and analyze their development over the course of the text; provide an evidence-based summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how they develop, and give a summary using evidence from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze what the text says explicitly as well as inferentially; cite several pieces of textual evidence to support the analysis.
description_plain: Your child can explain what a text says directly and indirectly, and use several pieces of evidence from the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 6-8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems written for grades 6-8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history.
description_plain: Your child can compare how a story or novel shows a time, place, or character with how it is described in real history, to see how fiction changes or uses history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a written story, drama, or poem to its audio, filmed, staged, or multimedia version, analyzing the effects of techniques unique to each medium.
description_plain: Your child can compare a written story, play, or poem to its audio, movie, stage, or multimedia version, noticing how each one uses different techniques.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author develops and contrasts the points of view or perspectives of different characters or narrators in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows different characters' or narrators' points of view in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a drama's or poem's form or structure contributes to its meaning.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the structure or form of a play or poem helps create its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative and technical meanings; analyze the impact of rhymes and other repetitions of sounds on a specific verse or stanza of a poem or section of a story or drama.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story or poem, including creative uses, and explain how sound patterns like rhyme affect certain parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular elements of a literary text interact.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different parts of a story, play, or poem work together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text; provide an evidence-based summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main theme or idea of a story and explain how it develops, giving a summary with evidence from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze what the text says explicitly as well as inferentially; cite several pieces of textual evidence to support the analysis.
description_plain: Your child can explain what a story says directly and indirectly, and use several pieces of evidence from the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about similar themes or topics, to learn more or see how different authors approach them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can outline and judge the arguments and claims in a text, checking if the reasoning and evidence are strong and relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information presented in different ways, such as in pictures, charts, or words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is said and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can examine how a text is organized, including how sentences, paragraphs, and larger sections connect to each other and the whole.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative uses, and explain how word choices affect meaning or tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how and why people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important supporting details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says, make logical guesses about it, and use specific examples from the text to back up their ideas when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multimedia and visual displays into presentations to clarify information, strengthen claims and evidence, and add interest.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, videos, or other visuals in presentations to make information clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and/or findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with relevant evidence, sound valid reasoning, and well-chosen details; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can give presentations that clearly explain their ideas, use good evidence, and speak clearly with good eye contact and volume.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and relevance and sufficiency of the evidence and identifying when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child can explain what a speaker is arguing, judge if their reasons and evidence make sense, and notice when something doesn't fit.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the purpose of information presented in diverse media and formats and evaluate the motives behind its presentation.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why information is shared in different ways (like videos or articles) and think about the reasons behind it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others and, when warranted, modify their own views.
description_plain: Your child can listen to new ideas from others and change their own opinions if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that connect the ideas of several speakers and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant evidence, observations, and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions that connect what different people are saying and answer others with good evidence or ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions and decision-making, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow group discussion rules, keep track of goals and deadlines, and help decide who does what in a group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready, having read or researched the topic, and uses what they learned to talk about the subject.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 8 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions, build on others' ideas, and share their own thoughts clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can adjust how they speak for different situations and use formal English when it's needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visuals to help explain their ideas and make presentations easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information and evidence in a way that is organized and easy for listeners to follow, using the right style for the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can judge a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and how well they use evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and evaluate information from different sources, like videos, charts, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can get ready for and join in conversations with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use grade-level vocabulary and look up new words when needed to understand or express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that mean almost the same thing but have different feelings or suggestions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use how words are related to each other to better understand what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech in context.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what figures of speech (like metaphors or similes) mean in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can double-check that they have figured out the correct meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses, and other general and specialized reference materials, both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses (online or in print) to find out how to say a word, what it means, or what part of speech it is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin word parts to help figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence or paragraph to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words or phrases based on grade 8 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clear up the meaning of unknown words or phrases using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs in the active and passive voice and in the conditional and subjunctive mood to achieve particular effects.
description_plain: Your child can use verbs in different ways (like active or passive voice, or to show possibilities) to create certain effects in writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use proper language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an ellipsis to indicate an omission.
description_plain: Your child can use an ellipsis (...) to show that something has been left out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation to indicate a pause or break.
description_plain: Your child can use punctuation marks to show a pause or break in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb voice and mood.
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix mistakes when the verb voice or mood changes inappropriately in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the indicative, imperative, interrogative, conditional, and subjunctive mood.
description_plain: Your child can use verbs in different moods (like statements, commands, questions, possibilities, or wishes) correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the active and passive voice.
description_plain: Your child can use verbs in both active and passive voice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of verbals in general and their function in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child can explain what verbals (like gerunds, participles, and infinitives) do in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of vocabulary for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech, how words are related, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unfamiliar words or phrases by using clues in the sentence, breaking words into parts, or looking them up in reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use language differently depending on the situation, and can choose words and styles that fit what they are reading, writing, or saying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child will use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child will use proper grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly, sometimes over several days for big projects and sometimes in shorter sessions, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw and cite evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence from stories or informational texts to support their ideas, reflections, or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from books and online sources, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information correctly without copying, giving proper credit.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer questions, using several sources and asking new, related questions to explore the topic further.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas efficiently as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, share, and work together with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new way, making sure it fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the topic, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that makes sense and reflects on the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can use specific words, details, and sensory language to help readers picture the action and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence, signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another, and show the relationships among experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words and phrases to show the order of events, changes in time or place, and how events are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, description, and reflection, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child can use dialogue, pacing, description, and reflection to develop the story, events, or characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view or perspective and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene, introducing the narrator or characters, and organizing events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, clear details, and a well-organized sequence of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that fits and supports the information or explanation they have given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain an appropriate style for content, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can use a writing style that matches the topic, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain or inform about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words and phrases to connect ideas and make their writing flow smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can develop a topic by using relevant facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories; include formatting, graphics, and multimedia when useful in aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, organize information into categories, and use helpful features like headings, graphics, or multimedia.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain a topic clearly by choosing, organizing, and analyzing relevant information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that fits and supports the argument they have made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain an appropriate style for content, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can use a writing style that matches the topic, purpose, and audience when making an argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to connect their claims, counterclaims, reasons, and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child can support their argument with logical reasons and evidence from reliable sources, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their main argument, explain how it is different from other viewpoints, and organize reasons and evidence logically.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their ideas with clear reasons and relevant evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly, sometimes for longer projects and sometimes for shorter tasks, for different purposes and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence from stories or informational texts to support their analysis, reflections, or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from books and online sources, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information properly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do both short and longer research projects based on focused questions, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, strong details, and a clear sequence of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas and information clearly by choosing, organizing, and analyzing the right content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that clearly support their opinions, using good reasons and enough evidence from what they've read or learned.

subject: Language Arts
