description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 6-8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand challenging nonfiction books and articles on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a case in which two or more texts provide conflicting information on the same topic and identify where the texts disagree on matters of fact or interpretation.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more texts on the same topic, find where they disagree, and explain the differences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; recognize when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child can identify and judge the arguments and claims in a text, deciding if the reasons and evidence make sense and noticing when evidence doesn't fit.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using different mediums to present a particular topic or idea.
description_plain: Your child can judge the pros and cons of using different ways (like videos, articles, or charts) to share information about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's perspective, and/or purpose in a text and analyze how the author acknowledges and responds to any conflicting evidence or viewpoints.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's point of view or purpose and explain how the author deals with evidence or opinions that disagree.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail the structure of a specific paragraph in a text, including the role of particular sentences in developing and refining a key concept.
description_plain: Your child can closely examine a paragraph to see how each sentence helps explain or develop a main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative uses, and explain how word choices affect the meaning and feeling of the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a text makes connections among and distinctions between individuals, ideas, or events.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a text shows connections and differences between people, ideas, or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to supporting ideas; provide an evidence-based summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea in a text, explain how it develops with supporting details, and give a summary using evidence from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze what the text says explicitly as well as inferentially; cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports the analysis.
description_plain: Your child can explain what a text says directly and indirectly, and use the best evidence from the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of grades 6-8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand challenging stories, plays, and poems on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a modern work of fiction draws on themes, patterns of events, or character types from myths, traditional stories, or religious works such as the Bible, including describing how the material is rendered new.
description_plain: Your child can explain how modern stories use ideas, events, or characters from old myths, traditional tales, or religious stories, and describe what makes them new or different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the extent to which a filmed or live production of a story or drama stays faithful to or departs from the text or script, evaluating the choices made by the director or actors.
description_plain: Your child can compare a movie or play version of a story to the original text and explain how the director's or actors' choices change the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how differences in the points of view of the characters and the perspectives of the audience or reader create such effects as suspense or humor.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different characters' points of view and what the audience knows can create suspense or humor in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the structure of two or more texts and analyze how the differing structure of each text contributes to its meaning and style.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more stories are organized and explain how those differences affect the meaning and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative and technical meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including creative or special uses, and explain how word choices affect the meaning and mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular lines of dialogue or incidents in a literary text propel the action, reveal aspects of a character, or provoke a decision.
description_plain: Your child can explain how certain lines or events in a story move the plot forward, show what a character is like, or lead to a decision.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to the characters, setting, and plot; provide an evidence-based summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main theme or idea in a story, explain how it develops with the characters, setting, and plot, and give a summary with evidence from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze what the text says explicitly as well as inferentially; cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports the analysis.
description_plain: Your child can explain what a story says directly and indirectly, and use the best evidence from the story to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging fiction and nonfiction texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts deal with similar ideas or topics and explain how the authors' approaches are alike or different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can identify and judge the arguments and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence are strong and make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information presented in different ways, such as through pictures, graphs, or words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is said and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how the parts of a text, like sentences and paragraphs, fit together and contribute to the whole.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative uses, and explain how word choices affect meaning or tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how and why people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the key supporting details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says and make logical guesses, using specific evidence from the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak audibly and express thoughts, feelings, and ideas clearly.
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly and share their thoughts, feelings, and ideas so others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions as desired to provide additional detail.
description_plain: Your child can add drawings or other visuals to what they say to help explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe familiar people, places, things, and events and, with prompting and support, provide additional detail.
description_plain: Your child can talk about people, places, things, and events they know, and add more details when asked.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions in order to seek help, get information, or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to get help, find out information, or clear up confusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Confirm understanding of a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media by asking and answering questions about key details and requesting clarification if something is not understood.
description_plain: Your child can show they understand a story or information they've heard by asking and answering questions about important details and asking for help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Continue conversations through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child can keep a conversation going by taking turns talking with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, follow agreed-upon rules for discussions.
description_plain: With help, your child can follow basic rules for talking with others, like listening and taking turns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about kindergarten topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child can join in group conversations with classmates and adults about kindergarten topics and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use proper English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, charts, or digital tools to help explain ideas during a presentation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information in a way that others can understand and follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about what the speaker believes and why.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can understand information that is shared in different ways, like through pictures, numbers, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can join in conversations with others, listen to their ideas, and share their own thoughts clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts.
description_plain: Your child will use new words they learn from talking, listening to stories, and reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs describing the same general action.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar action words, like 'run' and 'jog.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use.
description_plain: Your child can connect words they learn to things they see or do in real life.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of frequently occurring verbs and adjectives by relating them to their antonyms.
description_plain: Your child can show they understand common action and describing words by naming their opposites.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort common objects into categories to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can sort objects into groups, like putting all the animals together or all the foods together.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, explore word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help, your child will explore how words are related and what makes their meanings special.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring inflections and affixes as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word.
description_plain: Your child can use word endings or beginnings to help figure out what a new word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify new meanings for familiar words and apply them accurately.
description_plain: Your child can recognize when a familiar word is used in a new way and use it correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on kindergarten reading and content.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what new or tricky words mean by using clues from what they read or hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 2.
description_plain: This skill is taught starting in second grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell simple words phonetically.
description_plain: Your child can spell simple words by sounding them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write a letter or letters for all consonant and short-vowel phonemes.
description_plain: Your child can write letters that match the sounds in words, including all consonants and short vowels.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name end punctuation.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name punctuation marks that come at the end of sentences, like periods and question marks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize the first word in a sentence and the pronoun I.
description_plain: Your child can use a capital letter at the start of a sentence and for the word 'I.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete sentences in shared language activities.
description_plain: Your child can say and help make complete sentences when talking with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring prepositions.
description_plain: Your child can use common words that show where things are, like 'in,' 'on,' and 'under.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand and use question words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use question words like 'who,' 'what,' 'where,' 'when,' 'why,' and 'how.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Form regular plural nouns orally by adding /s/ or /es/.
description_plain: Your child can make regular plural words by adding 's' or 'es' when talking, like 'cats' or 'dishes.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring nouns and verbs.
description_plain: Your child can use common naming words (nouns) and action words (verbs).

subject: Language Arts
description: Print all upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can write all the uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words for school and everyday life, and try to figure out what new words mean on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand when words have special meanings or are related in different ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what new or tricky words mean by using clues from the sentence, word parts, or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use language in different ways depending on the situation and understand more when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 3.
description_plain: This skill is taught starting in third grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 4.
description_plain: This skill is taught starting in fourth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child can remember things they have learned or find information to answer a question.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, participate in shared research and writing projects.
description_plain: With help, your child can work with others on simple research and writing projects.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, explore a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help, your child can use computers or other digital tools to create and share their writing, sometimes with classmates.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, respond to questions and suggestions from peers and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: With help, your child can answer questions about their writing, listen to suggestions, and add more details if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 3.
description_plain: This skill is taught starting in third grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to narrate a single event or several loosely linked events, sequence the events in the order in which they occurred, and provide a reaction to what happened.
description_plain: Your child can use drawing, talking, and writing to tell about something that happened, put the events in order, and share how they felt about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose informative/explanatory texts in which they name what they are writing about and supply some information about the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use drawing, talking, and writing to share facts about a topic and name what they are writing about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose opinion pieces, in which they tell a reader the topic or the name of the book they are writing about and state an opinion or preference about the topic or book.
description_plain: Your child can use drawing, talking, and writing to share their opinion about a topic or book and say what they think or like.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will practice writing often, sometimes working on longer projects and sometimes finishing writing in one or two days, for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or nonfiction books to help explain their ideas or answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find information from different books or websites, check if it is true, and use it in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do simple or longer research projects by asking questions and learning about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use computers or the Internet to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can make their writing better by planning, making changes, fixing mistakes, or trying a new way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits what they are writing about and who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events, using details and putting the events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or teach about a topic, using facts and organizing their ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write to share their opinion and give reasons to support what they think.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child will join in group reading activities and understand what is being read.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: With help, your child can find things that are the same and different in two books about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can find the reasons an author gives to explain their points in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the text in which they appear.
description_plain: With help, your child can talk about how the pictures and words in a book go together.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the author and illustrator of a text and define the role of each in presenting the ideas or information in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can name the author and illustrator of a book and tell what each person does.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.
description_plain: Your child can point out the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about words they don't know in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can talk about how two people, events, ideas, or facts in a book are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell the main idea of a book and remember important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child will join in group reading activities and understand the stories being read.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in familiar stories.
description_plain: With help, your child can talk about how characters in different stories have similar or different adventures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the story in which they appear.
description_plain: With help, your child can talk about how the pictures in a story relate to what is happening in the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the author and illustrator of a story and define the role of each in telling the story.
description_plain: With help, your child can name the author and illustrator of a story and tell what each person does.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize common types of texts.
description_plain: Your child can recognize different kinds of books, like storybooks and poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about words they don't know in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify characters, settings, and major events in a story. Identify beginning/middle/end.
description_plain: With help, your child can name the characters, where the story happens, and the main events, including the beginning, middle, and end.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, retell familiar stories, including key details.
description_plain: With help, your child can retell familiar stories and include important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand both stories and informational books on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different books talk about the same topic or idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the reasons and evidence an author uses to support their ideas in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and talk about information shown in different ways, like pictures, charts, or words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how the author's point of view or reason for writing affects what is in the book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how a book is organized and how different parts fit together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a book and how the author's word choices affect the meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can talk about how people, events, and ideas change and connect with each other in a story or book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the main idea or theme of a story and tell you the most important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a story says and make smart guesses about it, using examples from the text to explain their thinking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read emergent texts with one-to-one correspondence with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read simple books, matching each spoken word to the printed word, and understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read emergent texts to develop fluency and comprehension skills.
description_plain: Your child can read simple books smoothly and understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode cvc words.
description_plain: Your child can sound out and read simple words like 'cat,' 'dog,' or 'sun.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between similarly spelled words by identifying the sounds of the letters that differ.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that look almost the same by noticing which letters and sounds are different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read common grade-appropriate high-frequency words by sight.
description_plain: Your child can quickly recognize and read common words like 'the,' 'and,' or 'is' without having to sound them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Associate the long and short sounds with common spellings for the five major vowels.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds and knows how they are spelled.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate basic knowledge of one-to-one letter-sound correspondences by producing the primary sound and the most frequent sounds for each consonant.
description_plain: Your child can match each letter to its main sound and say the most common sound for each consonant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about letters and sounds to read new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add, delete, and substitute individual phonemes in simple, one-syllable words to make new words.
description_plain: Your child can change, add, or take away sounds in simple words to make new words, like turning 'cat' into 'bat' or 'cut.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce the initial, medial vowel, and final phonemes in three-phoneme words.
description_plain: Your child can say the first, middle, and last sounds in simple words like 'dog' or 'sun.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend and segment onsets and rimes of single-syllable spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can put together and break apart the beginning sound and the rest of a simple word when they hear it spoken.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delete syllables in compound words with two syllables.
description_plain: Your child can take apart compound words with two parts, like 'sunflower' into 'sun' and 'flower.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Count, pronounce, blend, and segment syllables in spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can count, say, put together, and break apart the parts (syllables) in spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and produce rhyming words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and say words that rhyme, like 'cat' and 'hat.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and phonemes.
description_plain: Your child understands how spoken words are made up of smaller parts, like syllables and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name all upper- and lowercase letters of the alphabet.
description_plain: Your child can name and recognize all the uppercase and lowercase letters of the alphabet.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand that words are separated by spaces in print.
description_plain: Your child understands that there are spaces between words when we write.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that spoken words are represented in written language by specific sequences of letters.
description_plain: Your child knows that spoken words are written down using letters in a certain order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow words from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.
description_plain: Your child can follow words in a book from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print works, like how to follow words on a page and what letters and words look like.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child can speak in complete sentences and add details or explanations when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create audio recordings of stories or poems; add drawings or other visual displays to stories or recounts of experiences when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can record themselves reading stories or poems and add drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Tell a story or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking audibly in coherent sentences.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story or talk about something that happened, using clear sentences and important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to clarify comprehension, gather additional information, or deepen understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone else says to better understand or learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount or describe key ideas or details from a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can retell or describe the main ideas from something they heard or saw.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask for clarification and further explanation as needed about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask for more information or for things to be explained if they don't understand during a discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by linking their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can join in conversations by connecting their ideas to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions.
description_plain: Your child can follow the rules for talking and listening in group discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 2 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child can join group conversations about grade 2 topics with classmates and adults, sharing and building on ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on who they are talking to and use proper English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, charts, or digital tools to help explain their ideas when giving a presentation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and reasons in a way that others can follow and understand, using the right style for the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to someone speak and think about their point of view and reasons for what they say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information given in different ways, like through pictures, numbers, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can get ready for and join in different kinds of conversations, sharing their ideas clearly and listening to others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using adjectives and adverbs to describe.
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases they have learned, including describing things with adjectives and adverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among closely related verbs and closely related adjectives.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar verbs and adjectives, like 'run' and 'jog' or 'happy' and 'joyful.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use.
description_plain: Your child can connect new words to things they see or do in real life.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words are related and can tell the small differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries, beginning dictionaries, and other reference materials, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use a dictionary or glossary, in books or online, to find out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of the meaning of individual words to predict the meaning of compound words.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what a compound word means by knowing what each part means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root.
description_plain: Your child can use a familiar root word to help guess the meaning of a new word with the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known prefix is added to a known word.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of a new word when a prefix is added to a word they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the rest of the sentence to help figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 2 reading and content, choosing from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what new or tricky words mean by using different strategies, like looking at the sentence or using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare formal and informal uses of English.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between formal and informal ways of speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses proper language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child can use a dictionary, in books or online, to check and correct how words are spelled.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generalize learned spelling patterns when writing words.
description_plain: Your child uses spelling patterns they have learned to help spell new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an apostrophe to form contractions and frequently occurring possessives.
description_plain: Your child uses apostrophes correctly to make contractions and show when something belongs to someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in greetings and closings of letters.
description_plain: Your child uses commas in the greetings and closings of letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize holidays, product names, and geographic names.
description_plain: Your child capitalizes the names of holidays, products, and places.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce, expand, and rearrange complete simple and compound sentences.
description_plain: Your child can make, add to, and rearrange simple and compound sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child uses adjectives and adverbs correctly to describe things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the past tense of frequently occurring irregular verbs.
description_plain: Your child can use the past tense of common irregular verbs, like 'ran' or 'ate.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reflexive pronouns.
description_plain: Your child uses reflexive pronouns like 'myself,' 'yourself,' and 'themselves' correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use frequently occurring irregular plural nouns.
description_plain: Your child can use irregular plural nouns, like 'children' or 'mice,' correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use collective nouns.
description_plain: Your child uses collective nouns, like 'group' or 'team,' correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a wide range of words for school and everyday life, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands expressions that don't mean exactly what they say, and can tell how words are related and their small differences in meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what new or tricky words mean by using clues in the sentence, looking at word parts, or using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use language differently depending on the situation, and can understand more when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 3.
description_plain: This skill will be taught starting in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 4.
description_plain: This skill will be taught starting in grade 4.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: Your child can remember things they have learned or find information to answer a question.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects.
description_plain: Your child works with others on research and writing projects.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, use technology to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: With help, your child uses technology to write, share, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, focus on a topic and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing.
description_plain: With help, your child can stay on topic and improve their writing by making changes and corrections.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 3.
description_plain: This skill will be taught starting in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can end their story in a way that feels finished.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use temporal words and phrases to signal event order.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'first,' 'next,' and 'then' to show the order of events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to develop experiences and events or show the response of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses talking and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to show what happens and how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a situation and introduce a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can set up a story, introduce who is in it, and tell what happens in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events, using details and putting events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases to connect ideas within categories of information.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'also,' 'because,' and 'for example' to connect ideas when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, and details.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, and details to explain a topic when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic and group related information together; include illustrations when useful in aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic, groups related information together, and adds pictures if they help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write multi-paragraph informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child can write informative pieces with more than one paragraph to clearly explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply reasons that support the opinion, use linking words to connect opinion and reasons, and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write about their opinion on a topic or book, give reasons for their opinion, use words to connect ideas, and write a closing sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes,and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes often, sometimes taking time to plan and revise, and sometimes finishing in one or two days, for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses information from stories or nonfiction books to help explain their ideas or answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child gathers information from books and online sources, checks if it is trustworthy, and puts it together in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects by asking questions and showing what they learned about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child uses computers or the Internet to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by planning, editing, and making changes as needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and organizes their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events, using good details and putting events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or teach about a topic, using clear and organized information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child writes to support their opinions or ideas, giving good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in the grades 2–3 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand nonfiction books and articles for second and third graders, with help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points presented by two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find similarities and differences between two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how reasons support specific points the author makes in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the reasons in a text support what the author is saying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific images contribute to and clarify a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell how pictures or images help explain or add to the information in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main purpose of a text, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why an author wrote a text and what they want to explain or describe.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features to locate key facts or information in a text efficiently.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use things like headings, bold words, and tables of contents to find important information quickly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 2 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in nonfiction texts about second grade topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how events, ideas, or steps are connected in a nonfiction text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic of a multi-paragraph text as well as the focus of specific paragraphs within the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories and poetry, in the grades 2–3 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories and poems for second and third graders, with help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more versions of the same story by different authors or from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast different versions of the same story, even if they are by different authors or from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to stories or literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from the illustrations and words in a print or digital text to demonstrate understanding of its characters, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child uses both pictures and words in a book to help understand the characters, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge differences in the points of view of characters, including by speaking in a different voice for each character when reading dialogue aloud.
description_plain: Your child can notice when characters have different points of view and can use different voices when reading their words aloud.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure of a story, including describing how the beginning introduces the story, how the middle progresses the action, and the ending concludes the action.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a story is organized, including how it begins, what happens in the middle, and how it ends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how words and phrases supply rhythm and meaning in a story, poem, or song.
description_plain: Your child can explain how certain words and phrases add rhythm or meaning to a story, poem, or song.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how characters in a story respond to major events and challenges, including identifying the characters' feelings, the plot or problem, and how it is resolved.
description_plain: Your child can describe how characters react to big events or problems, including their feelings and how the problem is solved.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables and folktales from diverse cultures, and determine their central message, lesson, or moral.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories, including fables and folktales from different cultures, and explain the main lesson or moral.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand stories they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand both stories and nonfiction books on their own at the right level for their grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different books or texts talk about the same topic or theme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the reasons and evidence an author uses to support their ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and combine information from different sources, like pictures, charts, and written words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author’s point of view or reason for writing affects what is included and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child understands how sentences and paragraphs fit together to make up a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including special or unusual meanings, and explain how word choices affect the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how people, events, or ideas change and connect with each other throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea or theme of a story and tell how it develops, including key details that support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says and use details from the text to support their answers or ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or correct words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books out loud smoothly, with the right speed and expression, getting better with practice.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books with understanding and a clear reason for reading.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can read accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can recognize and read words that are spelled in unusual ways for their grade.

description_plain: Your child can read words with common beginnings (prefixes) and endings (suffixes) for their grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled two-syllable words with long vowels.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know spelling-sound correspondences for additional common vowel teams.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read words with common pairs of vowels (like 'ea' or 'ai').

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long and short vowels when reading regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in simple one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use phonics and word skills for their grade to sound out and read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Substitute and delete parts of blends in the final position in one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can change or remove the ending sounds in simple words to make new words.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can change the middle vowel sound in a simple word to make a new word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of spoken words in familiar two and three-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can say and work with the sounds in familiar two- and three-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: There is not a grade 2 standard for this concept. Please see preceding grades for more information.
description_plain: There is no grade 2 standard for this skill; please refer to earlier grades.

description: Make creative and strategic use of digital media in presentations to enhance understanding of findings, reasoning, and evidence and to add interest.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to manage the complexity of the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships among complex ideas and concepts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships among complex ideas and concepts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with well-chosen, relevant, and sufficient facts, extended definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with well-chosen, relevant, and sufficient facts, extended definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic; organize complex ideas, concepts, and information to make important connections and distinctions; include formatting, graphics, and multimedia when useful in aiding comprehension.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas, concepts, and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas, concepts, and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on addressing what is most significant for a specific purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on addressing what is most significant for a specific purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a style and tone relevant to the discipline in which they are writing.

description: Establish a style and tone relevant to the discipline in which they are writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships between claim(s) and reasons, between reasons and evidence, and between claim(s) and counterclaims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to link the major sections of the text, create cohesion, and clarify the relationships between claim(s) and reasons, between reasons and evidence, and between claim(s) and counterclaims.

subject: Language Arts

description: Develop and supply evidence for claim(s) and counterclaims, while pointing out the strengths and limitations of both.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 9, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 9–10 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range. subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 9, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 9–10 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range. subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
